verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B is the beta subunit of mitochondrial F1Fo ATP synthase, canonically resident in the mitochondrial matrix-facing catalytic core. However, ectopic cell-surface ATP synthase (including the beta subunit) is well-documented on tumor cells, hepatocytes, and endothelial cells, where it functions as a receptor for angiostatin, apoA-I, and other ligands. This ecto-ATP synthase pool has been validated by surface biotinylation, flow cytometry, and antibody-targeting studies, and has been pursued as an oncology target. The cancer/tissue-restricted ecto-display qualifies as cell-state-induced surface accessibility, not pure mitochondrial residency.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Magnitude and therapeutic tractability of the ecto-ATP synthase pool remains debated; could alternatively be framed as tissue_restricted_surface.
